name: Great Gluten Turkey
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons White wine or gluten stock
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the gluten flours, water, and flavor mix. The dough should be firm; add more flour if needed.
2. Roll the gluten into a rectangle and place it on the spread-out cheesecloth.
3. Loosely fold the edges over to meet in the middle to prevent sticking during cooking.
4. Tie the ends and around the middle with string, allowing for expansion up to 2-3 times its original size.
5. Place the wrapped gluten in a pot with water and seasoning; bring to a boil, then lower to simmering for 1 1/2 hours. Turn at least once during cooking. This can be done two days in advance.
6. Prepare your dressing and remove the gluten from the stock (for gravy). Unroll the gluten and fill with the dressing or mound it on the baking pan, tucking the gluten around it.
7. Reconstitute the bean curd sheets by soaking for 5 minutes in hot water; they will turn white and pliable but fragile.
8. Cover the cooked gluten roll with 2-3 layers of reconstituted bean curd.
9. Brush the "turkey" with oil or melted soy margarine between layers and on top.
10. Mix the wine or stock with the remaining oil or margarine and use this to baste while baking.
11. Add up to two tablespoons of seasoning mix for more flavor.
12. Place in a pre-heated 350°F oven and bake for 45 minutes to 1 hour, basting every 15 minutes. If it begins to brown too much, cover loosely with aluminum foil.
13. Carve and serve with stuffing.
Originally posted by Amanita (HURN) on Dec. 12, 1992/formatted for MM by DEEANNE
